test_cases.description: Test that the program fetches all users from JSONPlaceholder API and writes them to CSV correctly including all required columns.
test_cases.name: Successful API fetch and CSV file write

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API at /users returns a valid list of 10 users; disk is writable and CSV file can be created.
test_setup.then: CSV file is created with columns: id, name, username, email, phone, website, address, company. address and company columns contain JSON serialized objects. All 10 users are present. Logging contains summary info for each user.

test_cases.description: Test program behavior when JSONPlaceholder API is unreachable due to network issues.
test_cases.name: API network failure

test_setup.given: Network error occurs when HTTP GET request is made to /users endpoint.
test_setup.then: Program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating network/API fetch failure. No CSV file is created.

test_cases.description: Test program behavior when API returns an empty list of users.
test_cases.name: API returns empty list of users

test_setup.given: JSONPlaceholder API /users returns an empty array; disk is writable.
test_setup.then: CSV file is created with correct columns but contains no data rows. Logging contains no user summaries.

test_cases.description: Test program behavior when CSV file cannot be written due to file permission error.
test_cases.name: CSV file write permission error

test_setup.given: API returns users successfully; CSV file path is unwritable (e.g., permission denied).
test_setup.then: Program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating file write failure. No CSV file is written.

test_cases.description: Test program behavior when disk is full and CSV file cannot be written.
test_cases.name: CSV file write fails due to disk full

test_setup.given: API returns users successfully; disk is full and CSV file cannot be created.
test_setup.then: Program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating disk space/file write failure. No CSV file is written.

test_cases.description: Test program behavior when API returns a response that is not a valid JSON array of users.
test_cases.name: API returns invalid response format

test_setup.given: API returns malformed or non-JSON response (e.g., string or object instead of array).
test_setup.then: Program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating invalid response format. No CSV file is written.

test_cases.description: Test handling of user fields containing special characters, quotes, commas, newlines, and unicode.
test_cases.name: User data contains special characters

test_setup.given: API returns user objects with special characters in name, email, address, company, etc.; disk is writable.
test_setup.then: CSV file is created; special characters are properly preserved in CSV fields; address and company columns contain valid JSON serialization.

test_cases.description: Test that logging is properly initialized and summary info for each user is logged.
test_cases.name: Logging initialization and user summary

test_setup.given: API returns users successfully; disk is writable.
test_setup.then: Logging is initialized; summary info for each user is logged as per pseudo code; CSV file is created.

test_cases.description: Test behavior when some user objects returned by API have missing fields.
test_cases.name: User object with missing fields

test_setup.given: API returns user objects missing one or more fields (e.g., address or company); disk is writable.
test_setup.then: CSV file is created; missing fields are handled as null or empty in CSV; no mapping or validation is performed.

test_cases.description: Test program behavior when API returns a large list (e.g., 1000+ users).
test_cases.name: API returns large number of users

test_setup.given: API returns a list of 1000 user objects; disk is writable.
test_setup.then: CSV file is created with all users; performance is acceptable; all required columns are present.

test_cases.description: Ensure user data is written to CSV exactly as received from API, with no transformation or validation.
test_cases.name: No transformation of user data

test_setup.given: API returns users with various values; disk is writable.
test_setup.then: CSV file contains raw user data; address and company columns are JSON serialized as received; no mapping, transformation, or validation is applied.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users retrieves all users when UserRepository.fetch_all_users returns a valid list of User domain models.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_returns_all_users_successfully

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to return a list of 10 User domain models matching the JSONPlaceholder /users response.
test_setup.then: Function returns a list of 10 User domain models with fields: id, name, username, email, phone, website, address, company.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users returns an empty list when no users are available.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_returns_empty_list_when_no_users

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to return an empty list.
test_setup.then: Function returns an empty list.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users handles network error gracefully when API is unavailable.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_network_error

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to raise a network error (e.g., requests.ConnectionError).
test_setup.then: Function propagates or handles the error, terminating with a clear error message indicating API is unavailable.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users handles API failure scenarios (e.g., HTTP 500 or 404).
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_api_failure_status

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to raise an HTTP error (e.g., requests.HTTPError with 500 status).
test_setup.then: Function propagates or handles the error, terminating with a clear error message indicating API failure.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users correctly retrieves and returns a large number of users.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_large_number_of_users

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to return a list of 1000 User domain models.
test_setup.then: Function returns a list of 1000 User domain models.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users handles cases where UserRepository returns users with missing required fields.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_invalid_user_format

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to return User domain models missing required fields (e.g., no email, no id).
test_setup.then: Function returns raw data as-is (per requirements: no mapping, transformation, or validation), even if some fields are missing.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users correctly retrieves users with unicode characters in fields like name, address, company.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_unicode_characters

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to return User domain models containing unicode characters in their fields.
test_setup.then: Function returns users with unicode characters preserved.

test_cases.description: Verify that the overall service layer handles CSV writing errors (e.g., disk full, permission denied) and outputs a clear error message.
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_csv_write_error

test_setup.given: CSV writing function is mocked to raise an IOError (e.g., PermissionError, OSError).
test_setup.then: Function (or program) terminates with a clear error message indicating CSV file cannot be written.
test_setup.when: get_all_users is called and its output is passed to CSV writing.

test_cases.description: Verify that get_all_users handles cases where API response does not contain user data (e.g., empty response, wrong key).
test_cases.name: get_all_users_handles_no_users_key_in_response

test_setup.given: UserRepository.fetch_all_users is mocked to return None or an unexpected structure (e.g., {'error': 'not found'}).
test_setup.then: Function returns None or an empty list, per implementation; no transformation or validation is performed.

test_cases.description: Verify that fetch_all_users retrieves all users from JSONPlaceholder and parses them into User objects.
test_cases.name: Fetch all users successfully

test_setup.given: base_url is 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com', users_endpoint is '/users', API responds with valid user JSON array (10 users).
test_setup.then: Returns a list of 10 User objects, each with fields id, name, username, email, phone, website, address, and company populated from response.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when the API responds with an empty array.
test_cases.name: Fetch users with empty response

test_setup.given: API responds with empty JSON array [].
test_setup.then: Returns an empty list; no User objects are created.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when user objects are missing optional fields such as address or company.
test_cases.name: Fetch users with missing fields

test_setup.given: API responds with user JSON objects missing 'address' or 'company' fields.
test_setup.then: Returns User objects with missing fields as None or default values, as per domain model handling.

test_cases.description: Verify error handling when network error occurs during API request.
test_cases.name: API network error

test_setup.given: API is unreachable (network timeout, DNS error, etc.).
test_setup.then: Raises error; program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating network issue.

test_cases.description: Verify error handling when API responds with error status code (e.g., 404, 500).
test_cases.name: API returns non-200 HTTP status

test_setup.given: API responds with HTTP status code other than 200 (e.g., 404 Not Found).
test_setup.then: Raises error; program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating failed request.

test_cases.description: Verify error handling when API response is not valid JSON.
test_cases.name: API returns malformed JSON

test_setup.given: API responds with malformed JSON (e.g., syntax error in response).
test_setup.then: Raises error; program terminates and outputs a clear error message about JSON parsing failure.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when user objects include additional unexpected fields.
test_cases.name: API returns extra fields in user objects

test_setup.given: API responds with user objects containing extra fields not defined in domain model.
test_setup.then: User objects are parsed using only expected fields; extra fields are ignored.

test_cases.description: Verify error handling when base_url is invalid.
test_cases.name: Invalid base URL

test_setup.given: base_url is set to an invalid URL (e.g., malformed string).
test_setup.then: Raises error; program terminates and outputs a clear error message about invalid URL.

test_cases.description: Verify error handling when users_endpoint is incorrect.
test_cases.name: Invalid endpoint

test_setup.given: users_endpoint is set to a non-existent path (e.g., '/userrr').
test_setup.then: Raises error; program terminates and outputs a clear error message indicating failed request.

test_cases.description: Verify behavior when API responds with a large number of user objects (e.g., 1000).
test_cases.name: Fetch large number of users

test_setup.given: API responds with JSON array of 1000 user objects.
test_setup.then: Returns a list of 1000 User objects with expected fields populated.
